headline: Bankman-Fried’s $250mn bail
summary: US stocks sink after strong data support further monetary tightening
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Bankman-Fried’s $250mn bail’

Marc Filippino Good morning from the Financial Times. Today is Friday, December 23rd, and this is your FT News Briefing.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Sam Bankman-Fried had a hearing yesterday. The bail was set at $250mn. US stocks had a horrible year and some investors are pivoting back to bonds. Plus, we’ll look at why 2022 was the year of the “I, alone, can fix it” CEO.

Andrew Edgecliffe-JohnsonAnd it may be the turtle-necked founder or it may be the guy in the suit who thinks that they alone have the key that will unlock the problems of this company.

Marc FilippinoI’m Marc Filippino and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Sam Bankman-Fried went before a magistrate judge in New York yesterday. The founder of the failed crypto exchange FTX had an enormous bond set and an initial conference in Bankman-Fried’s case is scheduled for January 3rd. Here’s the FT’s US legal co
